summary: Decompiled the small lb/lblanguage module by replacing asm/melee/lb/lblanguage.s with src/melee/lb/lblanguage.c and adding a matching header. The code exposes simple language get/set and predicate helpers backed by lbl_804D3EE0[0] and by byte offset 0x16 from func_8015CC58(). obj_files.mk was updated to build the new C object instead of the assembly object. No PR body or review comments were present, so rationale and reviewer intent are not available beyond the diff.
what_changed: Deleted the 113-line assembly implementation at asm/melee/lb/lblanguage.s.
Added src/melee/lb/lblanguage.c with C implementations for func_8000AD8C, func_8000AD98, func_8000ADB4, func_8000ADD4, func_8000ADF4, func_8000AE18, func_8000AE58, and func_8000AE90.
Added src/melee/lb/lblanguage.h declaring lbl_804D3EE0, func_8015CC58, and the lblanguage functions.
Changed obj_files.mk so TEXT_O_FILES uses $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/lb/lblanguage.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/lb/lblanguage.o.
Implemented language range checks as language >= 0 && language < 2 before writing either lbl_804D3EE0[0] or func_8015CC58()[0x16].
Used ternary expressions returning 1 or 0 for the language-is-0 and language-is-1 predicate functions.
smart_moves: Kept the original autogenerated function names because no stronger semantic names were established in the PR evidence.
Modeled the small-data language state as extern u8 *lbl_804D3EE0 and accessed byte zero directly, matching the original lbz/stb assembly pattern.
Recognized that func_8015CC58 returns a byte-addressable object or buffer and preserved the observed byte offset 0x16 rather than inventing a struct field name without evidence.
Used explicit signed bounds checks for setters, matching the original assembly behavior that rejects negative values and values greater than or equal to 2.
Documented a matching workaround directly in the source: func_8000ADF4 matches but needs the correct epilogue patch, with scheduling disabled around that function.
decomp_lessons: For tiny accessor modules, direct byte-pointer indexing can be an acceptable interim match when the backing struct type is not yet known.
Do not force semantic enum or struct names when only raw language values 0 and 1 are evidenced by assembly; keep numeric comparisons until more context exists.
When migrating a file from asm to C, update obj_files.mk in the same PR so the build links the C object and stops building the asm object.
Predicate helpers that compile to branchy 1/0 returns may need explicit ternary return expressions rather than relying on C boolean normalization if matching is sensitive.
If a function only matches with a compiler/scheduling workaround, leave a precise comment identifying the remaining patch dependency.
naming_conventions: Header guard used lowercase underscored style: _lblanguage_h_.
Unidentified functions remained as address-based names such as func_8000AD8C and func_8000AE18.
Unidentified global data remained as address-based label lbl_804D3EE0.
The source/header names follow the lb module filename convention: src/melee/lb/lblanguage.c and src/melee/lb/lblanguage.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the assembly file only after adding C replacements for all exported functions shown in the assembly slice.
Matched lbz/stb accesses through u8 pointer indexing: lbl_804D3EE0[0] and func_8015CC58()[0x16].
Preserved setter control flow by guarding stores with language >= 0 && language < 2.
Used #pragma scheduling off/on around func_8000ADF4, with a source comment noting an epilogue patch requirement.
Returned explicit 1 or 0 from predicate functions to reflect the original branch-and-li assembly shape.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
There is no recorded reviewer feedback explaining the choice of prototypes, pointer types, pragma use, or header guard style.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_8000AD8C or other lblanguage functions to semantic names.
Find later PRs that define the return type or struct for func_8015CC58 and replace raw offset 0x16.
Search for use sites of lbl_804D3EE0 to determine whether language values 0 and 1 correspond to specific locales.
Search for similar #pragma scheduling off epilogue patch comments to identify reusable matching workarounds.
